description: This figure presents BF-STEM images of Type 3 dislocation structures in an austenitic Fe30Mn6.5Al0.3C low-density steel, comparing uncharged (a) and hydrogen-charged (b) samples after tensile deformation at 295 K. Subfigure (a) captures deformation bands (DB1–DB3) and dense dislocation walls (DDWs, dashed lines) within a grain oriented close to [6 5 8]//TA, whereas (b) shows coarser DBs traversing DDWs in a grain near [9 7 12]//TA. Images were taken under a two-beam condition using a (1 1 1)-type diffraction vector. Arrows indicate sheared DDW segments at DB-DDW intersections. Analysis reports that the hydrogen-charged specimen develops significantly larger shear offsets (~75–100 nm for DB3) compared with negligible offsets in the uncharged sample, and that the DBs are thicker (~0.5–1.0 μm versus ~0.3–0.5 μm) with individual bands merging into coarser structures. These findings illustrate hydrogen-enhanced strain localization and slip-band coarsening via secondary slip system (-1 1 1) [1 0 1] activity, supporting hydrogen-enhanced microbanding in austenitic steels.
